description_long: This apartment building features a modern, L-shaped structure with six visible floors, including a ground level that appears to accommodate commercial or communal spaces. The facade is primarily composed of red brick with large, regularly spaced windows and multiple balconies, providing outdoor space for many units. The building sits atop a raised, angular concrete platform that may serve as a parking or service area, and the roof is flat with subtle variations in height. The main entrance is located on the longer side, marked by a recessed area and a prominent doorway. The rear and side elevations are simpler, with fewer windows and no balconies, emphasizing privacy and structural solidity. The overall design is functional yet contemporary, suitable for urban residential neighborhoods.
description: A modern, L-shaped apartment building with red brick facades, multiple balconies, and a raised concrete base. The structure features six floors, large windows, and a flat roof, blending functionality with contemporary urban style.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main facade with numerous balconies, large windows, and the primary entrance set into a recessed area on the raised concrete base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the shorter wing of the building, with balconies and windows visible, as well as the angular extension of the concrete platform.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a plain brick wall with minimal windows and no balconies, emphasizing the building's solid and private back side.
description_view_3: Angled rear-side view showing the long row of balconies, the flat roof, and the extent of the raised concrete base.
attribute_window_arrangement: Windows are arranged in a regular, grid-like pattern with even spacing, aligned vertically and horizontally across the facade, and grouped with balconies on the main sides.
